title: East TN Business Plan - 2025  (3)

headings.text: Strategic Plan vs. Business Plan Objectives

headings_flat: Strategic Plan vs. Business Plan Objectives

sections_html.INTRODUCTION:
Purpose: The Business Plan has been developed by the IIBA Mentoring Team in order to provide Chapters with a disciplined approach to Business Planning. Chapters are free to adjust the template in any way they see fit for their Chapter, region or language. They are also free to use any other template of their own.

Inputs: Chapter’s Strategic Plan, Chapter’s Bylaws, and IIBA Strategic Plan.

Frequency: Generated annually to allow for adjustments or alignment to changing market conditions and new board members. Since the Business Plan provides a foundation for the marketing and financial plans, it should be reviewed prior to those planning sessions.

Participants: The entire Board of Directors and program leads for the Chapter. Each Board role should provide details for their contributions in the Business Plan.

Publication: The Business Plan should be available to all Chapter members in good standing but is typically summarized in Chapter communications with a link to the actual document provided.

Relationships: The Strategic Plan is used as an input to the Annual Business and Financial Plans.

The Business Plan has been developed by the East Tennessee Chapter in order to provide a disciplined approach to the management of the Chapter over the calendar year. For the East Tennessee Chapter, the Business Plan will be used to align board members, volunteers and partners with the goals of the organization and commit to the Chapter’s Mission and Goals through specific and measurable objectives. The Financial Plan should be reviewed against the Business Plan prior to committing finances for the fiscal year.
sections_html.GLOSSARY:
Term

Definition

Communication Channels

The different ways the board communicates with the members and why this is important. Describes the primary reason for channels, i.e., event promotions, elections, volunteer recruitment, and anything else that is important to the successful operation of the Chapter.

Essential Business Resources

The tools and services are needed to operate your Chapter. This should include technologies such as QuickBooks or SharePoint, as well as any other relationships with other groups inside or outside of IIBA.

Membership Characteristics

Members/member types, demographics, sponsors and/or corporate affiliations, educational and other institutional memberships, etc.

Revenue Sources

The main sources of revenue and the impact to your operations. This may also reveal areas of risk, and should describe plans to pivot if any of these become challenging.
sections_html.VISION: The vision for the East Tennessee chapter is to be the trusted hub for business analysis excellence—empowering professionals to connect, grow, and lead change within their careers and communities.
sections_html.MISSION: The mission for the East Tennessee chapter is to make business analysis accessible to all by fostering a collaborative environment where members can develop practical skills, build strong professional connections, and contribute to a dynamic community of practitioners.

sections_html.PRODUCTS AND SERVICES:
This section should speak to what products and services will be offered to members and which membership demographics will be interested in those offerings.

Membership Characteristics

  • Primary Member Segments:
    • Early-career professionals (ages 18–37)
    • Practicing BAs without formal titles (e.g., product managers, project managers, analysts in marketing, ops, hr, etc.)
    • Mid-career professionals seeking certification or career change
  • Secondary Member Segments:
    • Students and recent graduates (particularly from UT, Pellissippi, and local community colleges)
    • Career switchers from adjacent fields (e.g., IT support, QA, business operations)
    • Independent consultants and contractors
  • Affiliate/Partner Audiences:
    • Employers hiring for business analysis adjacent roles
    • Academic institutions seeking career pathways for students
    • City workforce development agencies and chambers of commerce
  • Member Needs Identified in Roadmap:
    • Accessible, affordable certification prep
    • Networking and peer connections
    • Real-world BA exposure (via events, mock interviews, shared tools)
    • Visibility into career pathways and advancement opportunities
    • Tangible value and community recognition

Essential Business Resources

Google Drive (Documentation, branding, shared templates)

IIBA Brand Assets (Marketing)

Zoom (Virtual events and meetings)

Revenue Sources

IIBA Global Membership Dues Share

Sponsorships (Employer partners, local businesses)

Event Revenue (Workshops, training series)
sections_html.Strategic Plan vs. Business Plan Objecti:
Element

Strategic Plan

Business Plan

Purpose

Define 3-year chapter-wide outcomes aligned to vision and mission

Define annual, role-specific goals that drive progress toward strategic outcomes

Timeframe

Multi-year (2025–2027)

One year (e.g., 2025)

Scope

Chapter-level goals across all focus areas

Individual board member or team responsibilities

Structure

Strategic Objectives (e.g., O1–O9), Strategies (S1–S9), Tactics

SMART Objectives aligned to strategies or tactics

Measurement

KPIs assigned to each Strategic Objective

Deliverables, timelines, and indicators of completion

Ownership

Shared by the entire board

Assigned to specific board roles, but may be shared between multiple roles.

Examples

“Improve certification readiness and success rates among chapter members”

“Develop and launch a pilot certification prep program leveraging curated training content and peer-led study groups by Q4.”
sections_html.Board Role: Board President:
Role Owner: Jordan Eisenback

Role Vision Statement

Describes the aspirational impact and leadership approach the board member brings to their position.

As President, I will foster a collaborative and inclusive environment where every board member feels empowered to lead with confidence and clarity. I will work to align our chapter’s operations with its strategic vision, maintain open communication with IIBA regional and global partners, and build strong relationships across the professional community. Above all, I am committed to ensuring that our chapter delivers meaningful value to its members, champions the practice of business analysis, and cultivates a vibrant, connected community.

Resources

What resources (people and tools) will be needed to be successful in this role?

People

Tools

Chapter Board Officers

IIBA Regional Directors and GCC

Chapter Sponsors and Partners

Volunteers

Chapter Google Drive to store chapter documentation

Chapter Zoom account to host meetings

Chapter Membernova


Objectives

The objectives the role owner is confident can be completed within the Business Plan year that map to the objectives and tasks defined in the Strategic Plan. Refer to the Tactics section of the Strategic Plan to use an input into your objectives.

NOTE: For objectives without stated tactics in the Strategic Plan (undiscovered at the time the Strategic Plan was created), map to an item in the Strategy section of the Strategic Plan. For estimated costs, only insert costs that are external to your organization (i.e. internal resources, free vendor gifts, etc.).

Role Owner: Warren Green

Role Vision Statement

Describes the aspirational impact and leadership approach the board member brings to their position.

As VP of Marketing, I will champion consistent, engaging, and inclusive communication that reflects our chapter’s identity and values. I will seek to raise awareness of our mission and programs across all platforms and foster meaningful connections with members, partners, and the broader community. My focus will be on amplifying member voices, promoting events, and strengthening our brand presence across East Tennessee.

sections_html.Board Role: VP Professional Development:
Role Owner: Sheri Smith

Role Vision Statement

Describes the aspirational impact and leadership approach the board member brings to their position.

As VP of Professional Development, I will create accessible learning opportunities that empower our members to grow in their careers and earn recognition for their expertise. I will collaborate with internal and external partners to design peer-led programs and alternative learning paths, while aligning offerings to diverse accessibility accommodations and certification needs.

sections_html.Board Role: Treasurer:
Role Owner: Jessica Lear

Role Vision Statement

Describes the aspirational impact and leadership approach the board member brings to their position.

As Treasurer, I will ensure that the chapter’s financial practices support its strategic goals through transparent budgeting, responsible stewardship, and clear reporting. I will work collaboratively with the board to align our financial plan with program priorities, seek opportunities to grow and diversify revenue, and maintain compliance with IIBA and local nonprofit requirements. I am committed to building trust through clarity and consistency, and to supporting chapter sustainability through sound financial leadership.
